title: Eskdalemuir seismic array: revised approach to managing onshore wind turbine interference
snippet: Proposals for an updated approach to managing onshore wind turbine interference with the Eskdalemuir seismic array.
ai_summary: DESNZ consultation on managing wind turbine interference with seismic monitoring; affects onshore wind generator viability and operational constraints in the UK.
ai_reasoning: Relevant to tem's wind generator customer base and potential grid stability impacts, but narrow in scope. A background read on UK wind policy rather than an immediate business driver.

title: Ofgem promises to regulate brokers without undermining the market
snippet:
What will TPI regulation mean and why Ofgem want your input into how it develops...

The post Ofgem promises to regulate brokers without undermining the marke
ai_summary: Ofgem is developing regulation for Third Party Intermediaries (brokers/TPIs) in energy markets. This could affect how tem engages with brokers, customers, and market dynamics in the I&C space.
ai_reasoning: TPI regulation is a material policy development for the energy supply sector. tem likely uses or competes with brokers; new Ofgem rules on broker conduct and market access could reshape competitive dynamics and require operational/compliance adjustments. Worth monitoring closely but not an immediate crisis.
